prompt_text: UserVoice Zendesk integration: how does it work?
response:
The built-in **UserVoice–Zendesk integration** adds a UserVoice app into the Zendesk ticket sidebar so support agents can log product feedback/feature requests from tickets directly into UserVoice ideas, link them to customers, and optionally subscribe those customers to updates.[2][3]

Here is how it works in practice:

**1. Where it shows up in Zendesk**

- The integration is a **Zendesk app** that appears in the **Apps sidebar on the ticket page** in Zendesk Support.[2][3]  
- It is only available on **ticket pages**, not globally across all Zendesk views.[3]

**2. What agents can do from a ticket**

While viewing a Zendesk ticket, an agent can:

- **Open the UserVoice app** via the Apps button in the top-right of the ticket.[2][3]  
- **Sign in with their UserVoice account** (they must have a **Contributor, Support, or Admin/full-access license** in UserVoice).[2][3]  
- Click **“Capture Customer Feedback”** in the app.[3]  
- **Copy/paste the customer’s words** from the ticket into the “Feedback” field in the app (the app does not auto-fill from highlighted text; manual copy-paste is required).[3]  
- **Search for an existing UserVoice idea** or **create a new idea** and link the feedback to that idea.[3]  
- Optionally **subscribe the ticket requester to idea updates** by checking “Subscribe customer to idea updates.”[3]  
- Save, which **logs the request in UserVoice** and links it to:
  - the **Zendesk ticket**, and  
  - the **ticket requester’s email address** (this email is taken from the ticket and cannot be changed in the app).[2][3]

The feedback text is stored privately in UserVoice and is only visible to your internal UserVoice admins/contributors, not to end users.[2][3]

**3. What the product/support team gets in UserVoice**

- Each captured request is associated with a **UserVoice idea**, accumulating **counts of how many customers requested it**.[2]  
- Product managers see a **centralized, aggregated view** of all feedback (from Zendesk and other channels) to identify trends and prioritize the roadmap.[2][4]  
- Agents who logged feedback or subscribed customers will **receive notifications** in UserVoice when the linked idea’s status changes (e.g., discovery → planned → in development → launched), so they can follow up with the customer.[2][3]

**4. What agents see back inside Zendesk**

- When agents return to a ticket, the UserVoice app shows the **live status of the linked idea** and **how many other customers have requested it**.[2]  
- This helps support communicate accurately about whether something is under consideration, planned, or shipped, without leaving Zendesk.[2]

**5. Setup / installation (high-level)**

In Zendesk:

- Go to **Settings → Apps → Marketplace** and search for **“UserVoice”**.[3]  
- Install the app and **enter your UserVoice subdomain key** (the part before `.uservoice.com`, or your custom domain if you use one; only the subdomain/domain, not the full URL).[2][3]  
- Optionally **set a display name** and **restrict access by Zendesk user role**.[3]  
- If you have multiple UserVoice subdomains, you can install the app **multiple times**, once per subdomain.[2][3]

In UserVoice / accounts:

- Ensure each Zendesk agent who will use the app has an appropriate **UserVoice license** (typically **Contributor** on current plans).[3]  
- If you add, remove, or change a **custom domain** in UserVoice, you must **update the integration configuration in Zendesk** to match the new domain.[3]

**6. Scope and limitations**

- Only available on **Zendesk Support tickets**.[3]  
- **No auto-highlight-to-feedback**: you must copy/paste from the ticket into the app.[3]  
- The **customer email is fixed** to the ticket contact in the app; you cannot override it.[3]  
- The integration is designed specifically for **capturing and tracking product feedback**, not for synchronizing full ticket histories or user profiles.

If you tell me your role (e.g., Zendesk admin vs. agent vs. product manager), I can outline the exact click-by-click workflow tailored to how you’ll use it.
